explanations.10: This tells mypy to ignore a specific type-checking error (attr-defined) that's giving false positives in this codebase.
explanations.12: This sets where mypy stores its cache files so it doesn't have to re-check everything from scratch every time.
explanations.15: This enforces that every function must have type hints (like def foo(x: int) -> str:) or mypy will complain.
explanations.17: This makes mypy stricter about Optional types — if something might be None, you have to explicitly say Optional[SomeType] instead of just SomeType.
